tasks.title: BBTT-W0-1: Gate relatedDocs on currentTaskId — briefing.ts:782-803
tasks.description: Council Executor's 60-min action. Wrap the relatedDocs population block in computeDocsContext (briefing.ts lines 782-803) with 'if (currentTaskId !== undefined)'. Today the only filter is 'task.id !== currentTaskId' which is satisfied trivially when currentTaskId is undefined, causing 20 attachments to ship for tasks the agent has no intent to touch. tasks.title: RCASD: ADR-069 + spec — governed execution unification, contracts at every boundary
tasks.description: Phase 2 RCASD planning. ADR-069 codifies governed execution unification — every interaction (one-shot spawn, multi-step playbook, HITL gate) flows through the same governed pipeline with the same contract enforcement. The Distributed OS metaphor from ADR-068 is fully realized: Playbook is the kernel; one-shot agents are short-lived processes. Resolver order shadowing is the load-bearing UX magic. Self-healing via meta-agent inject_into is the inflection point that makes the system enterprise-grade autonomous.
acceptance: ADR-069 written under .cleo/adrs/ with full RFC 2119 spec
Frame: from static orchestration to self-healing orchestration via meta-agent feedback loop
Canonical variable resolver order spec'd with explicit shadowing semantics: step ⊳ playbook ⊳ session ⊳ project-context ⊳ env ⊳ default
Return format JSON schema defined: { status: 'complete'
'partial'
'blocked'
'request_approval', manifest_id, ... }
AgentDispatchInput schema extended with optional bindings, allowedSkills?:string[], allowedTools?:string[] — backwards compatible
Inheritance contract spec'd: extends: keyword resolution chain (project ⊳ global ⊳ packaged) with override semantics
Synthetic-playbook contract for one-shot spawn: { name: '__synthetic_<taskId>', nodes: [{ id: 'main', type: 'agentic', ... }] }
Self-healing contract: on_failure.inject_into accepts meta-agent IDs (agent-architect, playbook-architect) for resynthesis
Decomposition into 10 child tasks with explicit acceptance criteria + dep graph

notes: 2026-05-07 03:57:26 UTC: 2026-05-06 OWNER CORRECTION: original AC spec'd a GRAPH_REPORT.md generator — WRONG primitive. nexus.db is global/cross-project queryable; static markdown goes stale and forces agents to parse text. Correct design: cleo nexus report emits a LAFS envelope composing existing nexus surfaces (hot-nodes, top-entries, clusters, hot-paths, full-context). Sections: god-nodes communities hot-paths top-entries suggested-questions all. Project flag --project <name>|. (current via project-info.json) or all (cross-project meta). Format flag: json (default) or md (thin renderer). Cached in nexus_cached_reports keyed on (projectId, sectionsHash, lastIndexedAt). The original AC reads 'GRAPH_REPORT.md generator (deterministic markdown summary)' — TREAT THIS AS DEPRECATED; implementation must follow the JSON-envelope design in this note. Static markdown rejected; md is render-on-demand only.

tasks.title: lint-migrations.mjs: add RULE-5 — fail build if multi-statement migration.sql lacks --> statement-breakpoint between consecutive SQL statements
tasks.description:
Add new RULE-5 to scripts/lint-migrations.mjs that catches the exact defect class found in T9163 investigation. CURRENT (verified): RULE-1 catches TRAILING '--> statement-breakpoint'; nothing catches MISSING breakpoints between statements.

PSEUDOCODE:
- Read migration.sql
- Strip line comments and block comments (DON'T strip strings)
- Count SQL statements (split on ;)
- Count occurrences of '--> statement-breakpoint' marker
- If statements > 1 AND breakpoints < (statements - 1) → ERROR

CI: existing migration-lint job already runs --fail-on=error in .github/workflows/ci.yml — no workflow change needed.

NOTE: lint must run cleanly AFTER T-A lands (not before). For independence in Wave 0, this task can land RULE-5 in disabled state (--enable-rule-5 flag default off) and T-G epic-completion task flips it on. Worker should choose the cleanest sequencing.
acceptance: scripts/lint-migrations.mjs adds RULE-5 (ERROR level): scans every migration.sql file; counts ; -terminated SQL statements; if >1 and no '--> statement-breakpoint' marker present between them, emits ERROR
lint-migrations --fail-on=error fails on RULE-5 violation
RULE-5 false-positive avoidance: ignores ; inside string literals and inside single-line comments (-- prefix) and multi-line comments (/* ... */)
GitHub Actions annotation output uses ::error for RULE-5 (consistent with RULE-1, RULE-2, RULE-4)
RULE-5 docstring in lint-migrations.mjs explains rationale (node:sqlite prepare() truncation) and links to T9163
At time of this task COMPLETE, lint-migrations on the repo passes (no surviving RULE-5 violations — depends on T-A having landed first)
Existing rules RULE-1 through RULE-4 still pass against the (post-T-A) migration set

tasks.title: perf(spawn): per-worktree node_modules bloat — investigate pnpm node-linker isolated or shared-modules strategy
tasks.description: Each ~/.local/share/cleo/worktrees/<projectHash>/<taskId>/ carries its own node_modules/ (~2.4 GB by du). On btrfs the underlying extents are reflinked into ~/.local/share/pnpm/store/, so 'du' wildly overreports the per-worktree footprint — 85 worktrees showed as 270 GB but cleanup yielded only ~12 GB real reclaim. The reflink savings are a btrfs-only accident; on ext4/xfs/macOS APFS the worktrees would consume 270 GB for real. Two strategies to investigate: (1) pnpm node-linker=isolated with a shared store-dir keeps the same disk savings without depending on btrfs; (2) symlink node_modules from a single 'shared' worktree node_modules into each task worktree, refreshed when package.json changes. Spike: measure footprint on ext4 baseline, then proposed strategy. Decision: which approach plus rollout plan.
acceptance: Spike report measures actual worktree node_modules footprint on btrfs vs ext4 for the same cleo orchestrate spawn
Two strategies (pnpm isolated linker, shared-modules symlink) prototyped and benchmarked for footprint and spawn-time cost
Decision recorded in an ADR or memory observation with chosen approach and rationale

tasks.title: Telemetry hot-path: buffered writes, opt-in audit, retention policy
tasks.description: telemetry.db is written by dispatch/middleware/telemetry.ts on EVERY CLI invocation. Under concurrent multi-process invocations this is N writers contending for one global DB. Today writes are synchronous and unbatched. Improvements: (1) buffer events in-memory, flush on process exit (or after N events / T ms); (2) audit opt-in default — confirm telemetry is OFF by default for new installs and explicit opt-in is required (privacy posture for an OSS dev tool); (3) document what's collected in the DB Charter (T9048) — fields, retention, anonymization; (4) define retention policy (rolling N days? max rows?) and implement rotation. Concurrency: synchronous=NORMAL is enough since per-event durability is not required.

Opt-in default is explicit and documented in --help and on first invocation

tasks.title: Cross-package DB-open drift: brain, studio, cleo, llmtxt-blob-adapter
tasks.description: Discovered during T9021 audit: at least 4 packages OUTSIDE core/store have their own raw new DatabaseSync() open helpers, completely bypassing applyPerfPragmas and openNativeDatabase: (1) packages/brain/src/db-connections.ts — full parallel implementation with getNexusDb/getSignaldockDb/getBrainDb/getTasksDb, no pragmas; (2) packages/studio/src/lib/server/db/connections.ts — same pattern, opens nexus + signaldock from SvelteKit server routes; (3) packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/agent.ts (lines 2169, 2864) and migrate-agents-v2.ts:311 — CLI commands opening directly instead of routing through core; (4) packages/core/src/store/llmtxt-blob-adapter.ts:216 — opens a 7TH database called manifest.db that wasn't on the topology map. ARCHITECTURALLY this means the @cleocode/core SQLite chokepoint is leaking — multiple packages reinvent the same primitives, drift in pragmas and lifecycle, and create concurrent-process scenarios (studio + cleo running together) that hit lock contention. The fix is to expose a single openCleoDb(role: 'tasks'|'brain'|'nexus'|...) helper from @cleocode/core and forbid raw opens elsewhere via a CI grep guard.
acceptance: All listed sites (brain/db-connections, studio/db/connections, cleo/commands/agent, cleo/commands/migrate-agents-v2, llmtxt-blob-adapter) either route through @cleocode/core's open helper or have a documented exemption
manifest.db is added to the canonical DB topology document (Database Topology memory) with scope and ownership
CI guard greps for new DatabaseSync( outside packages/core/src/store/ and fails if any new occurrence appears (extension of the T9025 lint rule)
Concurrent-process test: studio API route + cleo CLI command running simultaneously against the same project DB do not produce SQLITE_BUSY errors after pragma alignment

tasks.title: W6: Release 2 — DB topology split nexus.db into nexus-registry.db + nexus-graph/<projectId>.db, idempotent migration, ADR irreversibility, locking + handle-pool LRU
tasks.description: Wave 6 of Nexus restructure — RELEASE 2 deferred per Owner D2=D-then-A. Ships ONLY after R1 (W1-W5) is stable in production for at least 1 minor cycle. Splits global ~/.local/share/cleo/nexus.db into TWO files per Lead Beta: (a) nexus-registry.db (global, holds nexus_projects + project_permissions + cross_project_links + orphans tables — small file, registry-walk reads), (b) nexus-graph/<projectId>.db (one file per project, holds nexus_nodes + nexus_relations + nexus_communities + nexus_processes + nexus_hot_paths + fts indexes — graph-read I/O-local). Idempotent transactional migration with --rollback support; ATTACH+INSERT INTO copy from old; rename old to nexus.db.legacy.<ts>. Lock acquisition canonical order for hybrid ops: registry → graph(min(idA,idB)) → graph(max). Handle-pool LRU at packages/core/src/nexus/store.ts (soft cap 32) for cross-project queries against >100 registered projects. ADR-XXX records the split decision and the irreversibility profile (one-way migration; rollback only available within first release window).

--rollback flag re-merges legacy file back into nexus.db with deletion of split artifacts

tasks.title: Task 8
External leaf task
DocsAccessor: unified llmtxt + manifest interface (agent-outputs, ADRs, attachments)
tasks.description: Define DocsAccessor as the umbrella interface for ALL document operations in CLEO, wrapping the llmtxt SDK (llmtxt/sdk for AgentSession, llmtxt/blob for content-addressed storage, llmtxt/graph for KnowledgeGraph). Today the docs surface is scattered: docs/docs-ops.ts uses llmtxt/sdk + graph + similarity directly; store/llmtxt-blob-adapter.ts wraps llmtxt/blob with a different shape; sessions/agent-session-adapter.ts uses llmtxt/sdk with its own llmtxt.db at .cleo/llmtxt/; raw markdown agent-outputs in .cleo/agent-outputs/ are NOT in any accessor — they're filesystem reads. DocsAccessor should provide: storeDoc(content, kind, refs) for ADRs/agent-outputs/transcripts, getDoc(id|hash), listDocs(filters), searchDocs(query) (via llmtxt/similarity), exportDoc(id, format). Backed by llmtxt.db (sessions, receipts) + manifest.db (blob attachments) — both opaque to consumers. This task DEPENDS ON T1824 (Decision Storage Consolidation epic) and T1825 (ADR migration) — agent-outputs migration is a separate task, but DocsAccessor is the API surface they all converge on.

tasks.title: Registry-backed persona resolution — classify(task) consults agent registry DB
tasks.description: T891 as drafted classified via fs-scan of .cleo/agents/*.cant. That bypasses the registry DB which is the actual SSoT. Fix: classify(task) queries the agent registry (via agent-registry-accessor.ts) and returns the canonical agentId, then the composer loads the registered .cant file path from the registry row. Registry row becomes the single source of truth for {agentId, tier(global|project), cantPath, tools[], model, skills[], classification, attachment}. fs-scan is only used by cleo agent install/doctor to RECONCILE registry with disk, never by spawn. This makes the user-facing cleo agent commands authoritative — if you cleo agent detach X, X is no longer spawnable.

tasks.title: Governed Execution Unification — playbook-as-primary-orchestration, OpenProse parity, self-healing
tasks.description: Phase 2 of agent + playbook orchestration consolidation. Closes the 7 contract-enforcement gaps identified during T1929 RCASD: variable flow A/B, manifest-validated skills C, schema-validated return format D, per-step allowlist E/F, agent-initiated HITL G. Plus the architectural inversion: one-shot 'cleo orchestrate spawn' becomes a synthetic single-node playbook so EVERY spawn flows through the same governed pipeline (uniform audit, contracts always checked, canonical resolver order). Plus the inheritance pattern: extends: keyword on .cant and .cantbook for clean derivation. Plus the self-healing inflection: meta-agents (agent-architect, playbook-architect) wired into runtime feedback so iteration-cap failures auto-trigger resynthesis. Frame: this moves CLEO from static orchestration to self-healing orchestration, treating agents as managed compute resources.

tasks.title: Task 9
Migrate .cleo/agent-outputs/*.md raw markdown to llmtxt blob store via DocsAccessor
tasks.description: .cleo/agent-outputs/ holds 100+ raw markdown files today (audits, campaign plans, handoff notes, etc.). They're filesystem-managed (no manifest, no dedup, no search index, no tamper-evidence). With DocsAccessor (sibling task) this content should migrate to llmtxt-managed blob storage: content-addressed via SHA-256 (so duplicate handoffs dedupe), Last-Write-Wins manifest, integrity verified on read, optional Ed25519 signing via AgentSession. The migration is one-shot: walk .cleo/agent-outputs/, hash each file, store via DocsAccessor.storeDoc(content, kind:'agent-output', refs:{taskId, sessionId, agentName}), index for search via llmtxt/similarity, then move originals to .cleo/agent-outputs/_archived/ for one release as a safety net before deletion. Future agent-output writes go through DocsAccessor exclusively.

tasks.title: cleo agent install/attach → spawn integration (installed agents become spawnable)
tasks.description: Today: cleo agent install <.cantz> writes files and registry rows but spawn never reads them. cleo agent attach/detach toggles attachment flags that spawn ignores. Fix: wire the lifecycle events. On agent install: validate .cant parseable, extract tools/model/skills/classification, insert registry row with source=installed, copy .cant to the target tier dir (global or project). On agent attach <id>: mark attachment=attached in project scope, make agent spawnable in this project. On detach: spawn T### --agent <id> → rejects with fix hint (run cleo agent attach <id>). On remove: delete registry row + .cant file. Add cleo orchestrate spawn T### --agent <agentId> explicit override path that fails loudly if unknown.

install validates .cant — malformed → E_VALIDATION, no side effects

tasks.title: CANT DSL v3 — formal types + requires/ensures contracts + mental_model refs
tasks.description: packages/cant/src/types.ts today only types ParsedCANTMessage — the full agent DSL (kind:agent, skills[], permissions, transport, lifecycle, on Event, enforcement) is PARSED but NOT TYPED. Industry standard (OpenProse) requires contract-based semantics: requires/ensures declarations that the orchestrator enforces between steps. Introduce CANT DSL v3: (1) formal TypeScript types for CantAgent{kind, version, name, model, persist, role, parent, description, tone, prompt, skills[], permissions, transport, lifecycle, context[], events[], enforcement[], requires:Schema, ensures:Schema, mental_model:string}, (2) requires/ensures: JSON Schema or Zod schemas declaring input preconditions and output postconditions, (3) mental_model field referencing a markdown expertise module (packages/cant/src/mental-model.ts already exists — wire it), (4) migration script for existing .cant files (backfill empty contracts, infer types from existing fields), (5) forge-ts validation rules for new schema. This is the refactor per industry 'semiformal agentic DSL' standard — stops the TODO-stub problem visible in cleo-prime.cant today.

tasks.title: Playbook DSL (.cantbook) — state-machine runbooks composing agents + contracts
tasks.description: Industry standard (OpenProse, LangGraph, CrewAI flows): orchestration uses Playbooks/Runbooks — deterministic state machines that compose stateless agent invocations. CLEO today has NO playbook primitive — orchestrator hand-rolls wave execution in natural language. Introduce .cantbook (or .playbook) DSL: (1) nodes are either 'agentic' (spawn an agent with .cant persona + task) or 'deterministic' (run a tool/script, e.g. pnpm test, biome, cleo verify), (2) edges encode requires/ensures contracts from T903 — node B cannot start until node A's output matches B's requires schema, (3) built-in error handling: deterministic node stderr pipes back to prior agentic node with iteration cap (prevents infinite fix loops), (4) resume tokens at HITL gates (see T908), (5) externalized state in tasks.db.playbook_runs table (hash of playbook, current node, bindings, last stdout). The RCASD-IVTR+C lifecycle should itself be implementable as a .cantbook. Starter playbooks: rcasd.cantbook, ivtr.cantbook, release.cantbook shipped in packages/playbooks/. cleo playbook run <name> --input k=v command. This replaces the tribal-knowledge wave execution pattern with a deterministic, auditable, resumable runtime.
acceptance: packages/playbooks/ package with {schema.ts, runtime.ts, parser.ts, __tests__}
.cantbook grammar supports: node(agentic,deterministic), edge(requires/ensures), onError(retry, escalate, gate), resume_token, iteration_cap
cleo playbook run <name> command executes step-by-step with state in tasks.db.playbook_runs
3 starter playbooks: rcasd.cantbook, ivtr.cantbook, release.cantbook
deterministic node stderr → auto-injected into next agentic node's context (with iteration cap ≤3)
cleo playbook status <runId> shows current node, bindings, history
cleo playbook resume <runId> --token X continues from paused state
integration test: rcasd.cantbook runs end-to-end on T631 producing all 5 stages of research output

tasks.title: Thin-agent runtime enforcement (ORC rule: workers cannot spawn, max N tools)
tasks.description: feedback_orchestration_model.md documents: 'Subagents CANNOT spawn other agents. Only the main orchestrator thread spawns.' This is industry-standard 'thin agent' / 'inversion of control' per OpenProse — prevents context drift, runaway loops, and unauditable chains. Today the rule is honor-system only — nothing physically blocks a worker persona from getting Task/Agent tools in its permissions. Fix: (1) CANT v3 schema rejects permissions.Agent=true when role=worker (parse-time), (2) spawn payload strips Task/Agent from allowed-tools when role!=orchestrator (runtime), (3) registry row includes canSpawn:boolean enforced from role at install time, (4) attempt by worker to invoke spawn command → E_THIN_AGENT_VIOLATION with escalation hint (promote to lead role or return plan to orchestrator), (5) cleo agent doctor reports any registry rows violating thin-agent rule. Applies per ORC-006 (max 3 files) + new ORC-012 (workers have no Agent/Task tool). Enforced at both CANT parse + spawn runtime + subagent harness wrapper.
acceptance: CANT v3 parser rejects worker persona declaring Agent or Task in tools with E_PERMISSION_CONFLICT
spawn payload tool allowlist strips Agent/Task from role=worker
role=lead: Task tool allowed (return plan), Agent tool denied (orchestrator spawns)
role=orchestrator: Agent + Task both allowed
registry row has canSpawn boolean matching role
worker invoking cleo agent spawn → E_THIN_AGENT_VIOLATION
cleo agent doctor flags any rule violations
tests: 3 roles × spawn-allowed matrix + runtime rejection case

tasks.title: Resume tokens + HITL gates (OpenProse standard — pause/approve/resume for sensitive steps)
tasks.description: Per OpenProse + industry playbook standards: sensitive steps (publish, push, delete, external API side-effects) MUST halt the playbook and return a resumeToken that a human (or automated policy) approves before execution continues. Today CLEO has AcceptanceGate (post-ADR-051) + cleo safestop (context-limit graceful halt) but no HITL resume primitive. Fix: (1) playbook DSL (T904) gains requires_approval:true flag per node, (2) encountering such a node returns status=needs_approval + resumeToken:hash(runId+nodeId+bindings), (3) cleo orchestrate approve <runId> --token <t> --approver <who> records approval in tasks.db.playbook_approvals and unblocks, (4) cleo orchestrate reject <runId> --token <t> --reason records rejection and halts, (5) auto-policy hook: approved-tools list + approved-patterns can auto-approve (e.g. pnpm test always auto-approved, npm publish never auto-approved), (6) integration with cleoos doctor showing pending approvals. Addresses user directive (autonomous consensus with HITL fallback). Preserves agent agency while bounding blast radius.
acceptance: packages/playbooks/ runtime supports requires_approval:true node flag
hitting such a node returns {status:'needs_approval', resumeToken, nodeId, runId, preview}
cleo orchestrate approve <runId> --token <t> --approver <who> unblocks execution
cleo orchestrate reject <runId> --token <t> --reason halts with state preserved
cleo orchestrate pending shows all waiting approvals
tasks.db.playbook_approvals audit table (immutable)
auto-policy: packages/playbooks/policy.ts defines approved-tools + deny-patterns
integration with cleo safestop: context-limit triggers auto-suspend with resume token
tests: full pause/approve/resume cycle + reject cycle + auto-policy bypass

tasks.title: Conduit.db topology audit — project vs global, relocate ~/.cleo/conduit.db → ~/.local/share/cleo/
tasks.description: Investigation found conduit.db EXISTS AT BOTH tiers: .cleo/conduit.db (project, correct) AND ~/.cleo/conduit.db (global, wrong location). Global DBs SHOULD live at ~/.local/share/cleo/ per ADR-050 + existing pattern (signaldock.db + nexus.db are there). Having ~/.cleo/conduit.db at the XDG_CONFIG root rather than XDG_DATA is a drift. Also: purpose of the global conduit.db is undocumented — is it for cross-project agent messaging? Global agent-to-human conversation history? Determine and document. Fix: (1) audit current ~/.cleo/conduit.db rows — is it live data or abandoned?, (2) if live: migrate to ~/.local/share/cleo/conduit.db with path update in db-paths.ts, (3) if abandoned: remove and document reason, (4) document topology in docs/architecture/database-topology.md (project: tasks.db+brain.db+conduit.db, global: signaldock.db+nexus.db+[conduit.db]), (5) cleo doctor scan detects both old and new paths, prompts migration.

notes: 2026-05-04 20:44:33 UTC: 2026-05-04 OWNER REFRAME: this task is decision-only. The 98.2% noise figure rejects gitnexus's O(m²) all-pairs SPM module visibility synthesis (620 files × 619 peers ≈ 384k synthetic edges) — NOT Swift itself. tasks.title: T-MIG-LINT-CLEAN: Clean up 33 RULE-3 migration linter WARNs (pre-baseline snapshot gaps)
tasks.description: Pre-T1165 baseline migrations in packages/core/migrations/drizzle-{tasks,brain,nexus,signaldock,telemetry}/ have migration.sql but no snapshot.json. This is historically correct (snapshots were only generated starting with drizzle-kit integration mid-2026-03), but scripts/lint-migrations.mjs RULE-3 flags each as a WARN. Output shows 33 such [migration.sql | no-snap] entries across the 5 DB sets. Non-blocking (WARN not ERROR) but noisy and clutters CI output. Solution space (pick via research): (A) configure RULE-3 to grandfather pre-baseline migrations via a cutoff date or explicit allowlist; (B) regenerate snapshot.json for every historical migration (RISK: changes hashes, could break existing user DBs if reconciler scenario coverage is incomplete); (C) change the linter rule to treat missing-snapshot as INFO not WARN. This epic researches the options, picks one, implements it, and verifies zero RULE-3 WARNs remain on main.
acceptance: Research task identifies all 33 WARN entries by file path + DB set
Decision documented: which of options A B C is chosen + rationale (cite drizzle-kit behavior + reconciler compatibility)

tasks.title: Dynamic provider/model architecture — eliminate hardcoded model strings, build pluggable catalog
tasks.description:
# Dynamic Provider/Model Architecture Epic

## Problem

CLEO has ~10+ hardcoded model strings across at least 5 overlapping abstractions:
- Rust routing matrix (cant-router/src/router.rs:34-56)
- TS composer matrix (packages/cant/src/composer.ts:230-234)
- NAPI binding (crates/cant-napi/src/lib.rs:558 — flagged by Codex council, needs verification)
- Memory extraction defaults (packages/contracts/src/config.ts:307, packages/core/src/config.ts:101)
- Harness adapter defaults (packages/adapters/src/providers/{claude-sdk,openai-sdk}/spawn.ts)

Plus 4-5 partially-built abstractions that don't talk to each other:
- PiModelsConfig (packages/caamp/src/core/harness/types.ts:373-439) — user-editable catalog with CLI
- ProviderBackend (packages/core/src/llm/backend.ts:89) — transport-agnostic invocation interface
- cant-router::route_with_caps — typed cost/latency policy engine
- llm-backend-resolver warm chain — Ollama→transformers→Anthropic local-first cost floor
- model-provider-registry (packages/core/src/metrics/model-provider-registry.ts) — allegedly already uses models.dev (NEEDS VERIFICATION)

## Goal

Single pluggable provider/model registry fronting every LLM-touching layer. Support OpenRouter, Vercel AI Gateway, Ollama, models.dev as composite catalog sources. NEVER hardcode a model string in the source tree.

## Council decisions (both councils ran 2026-04-25/26, all 20/20 + 20/20 gates PASS)

### My council (Claude Opus, run 20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa)
- Recommendation: YES adopt unified registry, but FIRST move = role-taxonomy spec, not code
- Critical mitigation: do NOT dedupe Haiku (cost-bounded extraction) and Sonnet (OWNER OVERRIDE accuracy-mandate) literals into one registry key — they encode contradictory constraints
- 4-fifths exists already, only the role/intent layer is missing
- Confidence: HIGH

### Codex council (run /tmp/council-router-provider-audit-20260426 → preserved at .cleo/agent-outputs/T-PROVIDER-MODEL-CODEX-COUNCIL/)
- Recommendation: 5-axis separation of concerns
  - catalogProvider (openrouter | vercel-gateway | ollama | models-dev | static)
  - invocationTransport (anthropic | openai | openai-compatible | gemini | vercel-gateway)
  - agentHarnessProvider (claude-code | opencode | pi | cursor)
  - RoutingConfig (policy only — tier budgets, preferred refs, fallback refs)
  - ModelRef (provider-qualified identity + invocation metadata)
- Composite catalog recommended: OpenRouter + Vercel AI Gateway + Ollama + models.dev
- Code in packages/contracts (shared types), packages/core (catalog + validation), crates/cant-router + packages/cant (pure policy-fed routing), adapters consume routed ModelRef

## Reconciliation (open question for next session)

Both councils compatible? My role-taxonomy lives ABOVE Codex's 5 axes (a 6th axis: role-intent), or INSIDE RoutingConfig as policy keys? Codex rescue agent was dispatched to write the unified design note but HUNG mid-write (cancelled task-mof5v4pw-457y1m at ~02:50 UTC). Design note PROVIDER-MODEL-ARCHITECTURE-DESIGN-2026-04-26.md was NEVER WRITTEN — Codex got through investigation (verified surfaces, ran ~50 commands) then stalled before producing output.

## Artifacts (all paths absolute under /mnt/projects/cleocode/)

### My council (Opus)
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/output.md — full transcript (75KB, all 5 advisors + 5 peer reviews + Phase 2.5 + Chairman)
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/verdict.md — Chairman synthesis (~80 lines)
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/tldr.md — recommendation + action + confidence
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/phase0.md — evidence pack (7 items, file:line citations)
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/phase1-{contrarian,first-principles,expansionist,outsider,executor}.md
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/peer-{contrarian-on-first-principles,first-principles-on-expansionist,expansionist-on-outsider,outsider-on-executor,executor-on-contrarian}.md
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/phase2_5.md — convergence check (no flag)
- .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/phase3.md — Chairman verdict

### Codex council
- .cleo/agent-outputs/T-PROVIDER-MODEL-CODEX-COUNCIL/provider-model-surface-map.md — Codex's surface map (KEY ARTIFACT)
- .cleo/agent-outputs/T-PROVIDER-MODEL-CODEX-COUNCIL/phase0.md through phase3.md — Codex council phases
- .cleo/agent-outputs/T-PROVIDER-MODEL-CODEX-COUNCIL/peer-*.md — Codex peer reviews

### Related context (memory + recent release)
- ~/.claude/projects/-mnt-projects-cleocode/memory/spawn-capability-gap-2026-04-25.md — sonnet/cleo-subagent default-model spawn failure mode
- Commit 7dc18d9e7 (v2026.4.149) — Worker model haiku→sonnet across orchestrator instructions (the instruction-layer fix that preceded this epic)

## Next session entry points

1. Read this epic description first.
2. Read .cleo/council-runs/20260426T021510Z-bba7abaa/verdict.md (Opus council verdict, ~80 lines).
3. Read .cleo/agent-outputs/T-PROVIDER-MODEL-CODEX-COUNCIL/phase3.md (Codex council verdict).
4. Read .cleo/agent-outputs/T-PROVIDER-MODEL-CODEX-COUNCIL/provider-model-surface-map.md (Codex's surface enumeration — most actionable single artifact).
5. Verify Codex's three new-surface claims: cant-napi/src/lib.rs:558, model-provider-registry.ts (does it already use models.dev?), operations/llm.ts:12 (ModelTransport too narrow).
6. Either: (a) re-run Codex with tighter "skip investigation, write design note" prompt, OR (b) write the unified design note manually combining both council verdicts.

## Open questions for owner

1. Telemetry shape: should ModelSelection emit per-tier/per-provider cost telemetry at design time? (Expansionist's billing/observability spine.) One column now vs. 7-layer retrofit later.
2. Vercel AI SDK convergence: should ProviderBackend collapse onto Vercel AI SDK at the cost of structured-output.ts prompt-caching + repair features? Or preserve documented orthogonality at core/llm/index.ts:5-7?
3. Composite catalog tie-break: when OpenRouter says context_window=200k and models.dev says 128k for the same model id, which wins? (Codex council recommended composite but didn't decide tie-break policy.)
4. Role-taxonomy placement: above the 5-axis separation as a 6th axis, or inside RoutingConfig as policy keys?

## Acceptance criteria (epic-level)

- Unified design note at .cleo/agent-outputs/PROVIDER-MODEL-ARCHITECTURE-DESIGN-2026-04-26.md (or later-dated) reconciling both councils
- Role-taxonomy spec defined (cost-bounded | accuracy-mandate | tier-policy | per-spawn override) with explicit constraint per role
- Phased migration plan: Phase 0 spec → Phase 1 contracts → Phase 2 catalog adapters → Phase 3 routing refactor → Phase 4 cleanup, with go/no-go gates per phase
- Zero hardcoded model strings remain in source tree (verified by ripgrep CI gate)
- catalogProvider abstraction supports at least 2 sources (e.g. OpenRouter + static fixture) with documented extension points for Ollama and Vercel AI Gateway
acceptance: Unified design note written reconciling Opus + Codex councils
Role-taxonomy spec defined with explicit constraint per role
Phased migration plan with go/no-go gates per phase
Zero hardcoded model strings in source tree (ripgrep CI gate green)
catalogProvider supports ≥2 sources with documented extension points

tasks.title: T-FOUNDATION-LOCKDOWN: project-agnostic anti-drift enforcement layer for CLEO harness
tasks.description: CLEO is a harness toolset for LLM agents working on ANY project. Currently CLEO tracks tasks well but doesn't ENFORCE the lifecycle programmatically — drift happens at known leak points (worker self-reports trusted, AC reframed mid-flight, work shipped without task binding, handoff overwrites lose history, cherry-pick destroys provenance, etc). This epic adds project-agnostic enforcement so drift becomes IMPOSSIBLE without an explicit governance-tracked override. ALL children must be project-agnostic (no cleocode-specific assumptions). Source of truth for project state must come from TASKS + BRAIN ONLY — markdown handoffs are deprecated as canonical. Worktree integration must use git merge --no-ff to preserve commit provenance (cherry-pick doctrine retired 2026-04-29 — see memory feedback_cherry_pick_worktrees.md).

tasks.title: T-LLM-WIRING: Wire real LLM intelligence into sentient/dream/daemon — multi-provider, tiered escalation, auto-start
tasks.description: Real LLM-powered intelligence for the sentient harness, replacing the rule-based scaffolding shipped in v2026.5.1. Six parallel workstreams: (1) centralize credentials resolver across the codebase so EVERY LLM consumer uses one path (env, ~/.claude/.credentials.json, config-driven provider selection); (2) add Moonshot Kimi backend (Kimi K2 coding model) alongside Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini; (3) tiered escalation in hygiene scans (filesystem → SQL → Jaccard → LLM only when ambiguous); (4) real dream cycle that feeds BRAIN observations to LLM for pattern synthesis; (5) tiered semantic duplicate detection (BM25 → Jaccard → LLM); (6) auto-register daemon as systemd user unit (Linux) / launchd plist (macOS) on cleo install. Cost discipline: only escalate to LLM when deterministic tiers cannot decide. Confidence-gated HITL: low-confidence proposals always route to owner; high-confidence non-destructive auto-execute; destructive always-HITL.

tasks.title: SQLite open-site centralization sweep
tasks.description: Sweep the ~17 remaining new DatabaseSync() call sites in packages/core/src that bypass the canonical pragma helper in store/sqlite-pragmas.ts. Today only the chokepoints (sqlite-native, conduit-sqlite, init, seed-install, local-transport) apply the full perf pragma set; the rest fall back to SQLite defaults (~2 MB cache, no mmap, default synchronous=FULL, inconsistent busy_timeout). tasks.title: CLI startup tax reduction (runStartupMaintenance + DDL/migration replay)
tasks.description: Every non-fast-path cleo command runs runStartupMaintenance() in packages/cleo/src/cli/index.ts, which: (1) detectAndRemoveLegacyGlobalFiles, (2) detectAndRemoveStrayProjectNexus, (3) needsSignaldockToConduitMigration check + migrate, (4) ensureConduitDb (opens conduit.db, runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS DDL chain, runs Drizzle migration reconciliation, writes _conduit_meta sentinel), (5) ensureGlobalSignaldockDb (same pattern), (6) validateGlobalSalt. Steps 4-5 are idempotent but execute SQL on every CLI invocation against a stable schema — the DDL replay and migration journal reconciliation are pure overhead once the DB is at current schema. The recent T9011 startup work showed this contributes meaningfully to wall-time variance under contention. Addressing it requires a schema_version sentinel pattern (skip DDL/migrate if persisted version == code constant) plus moving as much as possible behind a 'first command this session' gate.
acceptance: Schema-version sentinel: ensureConduitDb skips applyConduitSchema + runConduitMigrations when _conduit_meta.schema_version equals CONDUIT_SCHEMA_VERSION code constant — saves DDL and Drizzle journal scan on every steady-state invocation
Same sentinel pattern applied to ensureGlobalSignaldockDb (signaldock.db has its own schema version sentinel via __drizzle_migrations latest hash)

legacy-cleanup steps 1-2 (detectAndRemoveLegacyGlobalFiles, detectAndRemoveStrayProjectNexus) gated behind a one-shot marker file (.cleo/.startup-cleanup-done-<hash>) so they fire once per machine per code version, not every invocation
Benchmark: median wall time for cleo find foo on a populated project drops by ≥ 30% measured via 50-run timing harness (rule out variance from system load by capturing p50/p95)

HOT-PATH evidence: under concurrent multi-process invocations (3x cleo find / cleo show / cleo next loops in parallel), p99 latency drops measurably — the goal is making startup cheap enough that contention stops dominating
All existing tests pass; no regression in fresh-DB / fresh-checkout flows (the sentinel must correctly fall through to full DDL when sentinel is absent)

tasks.title: Cloud sync: namespaced multi-tenant PostgreSQL backend for cross-machine CLEO project sync
tasks.description: CLEO is local-first SQLite for portability and offline operation, but multiple-developer / multiple-machine workflows need a sync layer. This epic defines and builds a cloud-sync backend using PostgreSQL with project namespacing, where each CLEO project gets a logical namespace (schema or row-level tenant key) inside a shared Postgres cluster, and SQLite locally pushes/pulls to that namespace. Multi-tenant support means many CLEO projects coexist in one Postgres instance without cross-contamination, with row-level security or schema isolation enforcing the boundary. Architecture builds on the umbrella DataAccessor pattern (T9050) — the DataAccessor interface is engine-neutral, so a PostgresDataAccessor can be a drop-in replacement for SqliteDataAccessor. Rust signaldock-storage already has a postgres feature flag in Cargo.toml — that work is the cloud-side counterpart and must stay aligned. The sync semantics need to be defined: full-replace? CRDT-merge (cr-sqlite already in T947 as optional peer)? Last-write-wins per row? Pull-only / push-only / bidirectional? Conflict resolution? Out of scope: realtime collaboration (CRDT live editing) — that's a separate project under llmtxt-graph.
acceptance: Sync model decision: bidirectional pull/push, conflict resolution, identity verification — all documented in an ADR before implementation
PostgresDataAccessor implementation in @cleocode/core implements the same DataAccessor interface as SqliteDataAccessor — proves engine-neutral abstraction (T9050) is real
Multi-tenant namespacing: a single Postgres instance hosts ≥2 CLEO projects with no cross-tenant data leakage — verified by integration test
cleo sync push and cleo sync pull commands implemented; cleo sync status shows divergence
Optional cr-sqlite-style merge for brain.db (cross-device knowledge sharing) opt-in per ADR-? from T947
Auth + authorization model: each tenant has an identity (signaldock identity), each push signed with Ed25519 receipt (llmtxt/sdk pattern), Postgres rejects unsigned pushes
Backup story: postgres-side backup integrates with backup-pack so a CLEO restore can pull from cloud OR local snapshot
Cost / scale model: estimate Postgres tenancy density (projects per cluster) and document operating cost per tenant

tasks.title: BUG: Project hash drift — same project resolving to TWO different hashes (4f2a513f66dcb422 vs 1e3146b7352ba279) — must be deterministic + registered per-project
tasks.description: Owner observation 2026-05-05: /mnt/projects/cleocode has 2 active worktree hash dirs simultaneously: 4f2a513f66dcb422 (used by Wave 0 T1815/T1845/T1857/T1865) and 1e3146b7352ba279 (used by T-spec-flake, T1878, T1880, T1882-merged). Project hash is supposed to be deterministic per ADR-055/T1140 (D029 canonical layout via env-paths) — same project root → same hash forever. Drift means workers and orchestrator can't reliably locate ONE project's worktrees. Likely cause: hash function input differs (e.g., realpath vs path-with-symlinks, or trailing slash normalization, or @cleocode/paths SSoT migration in T1882 changed the algorithm without backfill). Fix: (a) audit the projectHash computation function (probably moved to @cleocode/paths after T1882); (b) require canonical projectInfo registration on cleo init that stores the hash IMMUTABLY in .cleo/project-info.json; (c) read hash from project-info.json everywhere, NEVER recompute from path; (d) detect mismatch on cleo doctor + warn + offer migrate. The 1e3146b7352ba279 dir is the newer one — likely the canonical going forward; 4f2a513f66dcb422 is legacy and needs migration.
acceptance: projectHash function audited and unified at single SSoT in @cleocode/paths
.cleo/project-info.json stores immutable projectHash on init, read-only thereafter
all hash consumers read from project-info.json not recompute
cleo doctor detects + reports hash mismatch when path-based recompute disagrees with stored value
migration script consolidates 4f2a513f66dcb422 → 1e3146b7352ba279 (or whichever is canonical) for /mnt/projects/cleocode

tasks.title: ADR-051 + ADR-062 ordering bug: evidence validation runs BEFORE merge, fails commit-reachability check
tasks.description: Pattern observed across T1862, T1830 (and likely more): workers spawn from main snapshot, do work, commit on task/T<id>, capture commit:<sha> evidence. cleo complete then runs validation: ADR-051 evidence-stale check looks for commit reachable from main HEAD. Commit ISN'T reachable until ADR-062 merge runs. Result: cleo complete fails before it gets to merge, blocking close-out. Workers who legitimately finished work hit a wall they didn't author. Fix: invert order — ADR-062 merge first, ADR-051 evidence validation after merge (commit will be reachable). OR: evidence validation accepts commits from any task/* branch reachable across all branches (git rev-list --all). Discovered while running T1862 + T1830 recovery — both required manual rebase to make commits reachable from main HEAD. Workers correctly refused OWNER_OVERRIDE per directive; orchestrator had to dispatch recovery workers.
acceptance: cleo complete invokes ADR-062 merge BEFORE ADR-051 evidence-reachability check (or alternative: validation accepts task/* branch commits)
integration test reproduces parallel-worker stale-base scenario (multiple workers spawn from common base, merge in sequence)
workers no longer require manual rebase before cleo complete after parallel main movement
test: spawn 3 workers in parallel, all complete via cleo complete without manual rebase intervention

tasks.title: BUG: Worker spawned task/T<id> branches inherit orphan history (test fixtures, unrelated commits) — must always start from clean main
tasks.description: Pattern: worker prompts say 'git worktree add ... -b task/T<id> main' to create new branch from main. But task/T<id> may already exist from prior session OR from T1878 worktree provisioner integration tests (which leave fixture branches like 'add main-only.txt' commits). When worker creates worktree with -b on existing branch, it reuses the existing branch (not main). On merge, brings orphan history (a.txt, exp-only.txt, README.md regressions). Discovered via T1817 merge accidentally introducing 277 lines of garbage; reverted + cherry-picked clean. T1846 had same pattern. Fix: (a) worker prompts must run 'git branch -D task/T<id> || true; git branch task/T<id> main' BEFORE worktree add; (b) T1878 integration tests must clean up fixture branches after run; (c) cleo orchestrate spawn provisioner should detect orphan-base task/T<id> branches (task branch with commits not reachable from main) and refuse to use them.
acceptance: Worker prompt template includes branch-cleanup before git worktree add

tasks.title: EPIC: Steal-from-graphify capability set + token-cheap LLM enrichment ladder — beat graphify on cost while matching/exceeding agent-token-reduction value

tasks.title: BUG: cleo init pollutes global agent registry with project-tier rows pinned to absolute paths
tasks.description:
USER-REPORTED 2026-05-07. During schema-drift investigation, an Explore agent ran 'cleo init' in /tmp/cleo-fresh-0n72Q/ then rm -rf'd it. The 5 default project-tier agents (project-code-worker, project-dev-lead, project-docs-worker, project-orchestrator, project-security-worker) registered in the global agent registry survived the cleanup. They are now surfacing as resolver WARNs during cleo orchestrate spawn from /mnt/projects/cleocode (different project hash 1e3146b7352ba279):

[agent-resolver] WARN: orphan row for agent_id='project-dev-lead' at tier='project': cant_path='/tmp/cleo-fresh-0n72Q/.cleo/cant/agents/project-dev-lead.cant' no longer exists.

cleo agent doctor confirms 5 D-002 ERRORs. cleo agent remove fails with E_NOT_FOUND ('not attached to current project') — so the orphans cannot be cleaned from our working directory at all. They're leaking into our resolver despite belonging to a different (dead) project.

ROOT CAUSES TO INVESTIGATE:
1. cleo init writes to global signaldock.db with absolute path baked in — should be relative-to-project OR project-hash-scoped
2. agent-resolver tier=project lookup may not properly scope by project_hash — pulling in any project-tier row regardless of which project owns it
3. No project-lifecycle hook to clean registry rows when project dir is deleted

RELATED: T1891 (project hash drift between 4f2a513f66dcb422 vs 1e3146b7352ba279) — same project resolves differently in different contexts. Likely contributes.
acceptance: cleo init no longer registers 5 project-tier agents in the global signaldock.db registry using absolute cant_paths that won't survive directory deletion

OR — registry rows include project_hash + abs path, and the agent-resolver filters by current project_hash so cross-project leak cannot surface (today: orphans from /tmp/cleo-fresh-0n72Q surface during spawns from /mnt/projects/cleocode)
cleo agent doctor adds --repair flag that auto-prunes orphan rows whose cant_path no longer exists (currently doctor reports the issue but cannot fix it)
cleo agent remove gains a --global flag (or new command cleo agent prune-orphans) so dead-project rows can be cleaned from any working directory
Repro test: create tmpdir, run cleo init in tmpdir, rm -rf tmpdir, run cleo agent doctor from another project — must show zero orphan WARN
Existing T1891 (Project hash drift) findings should inform the fix: ensure consistent project_hash derivation

tasks.title: BUG: cleo complete destroys task worktree+branch before integration — orphans completed work
tasks.description:
USER-DETECTED 2026-05-07 during T9163 schema-drift epic execution. Worker T9166 completed successfully with verified gates (51 tests, biome+tsc clean) and committed 3090a75b1 to task/T9166 branch. After cleo complete, the worktree at /home/keatonhoskins/.local/share/cleo/worktrees/1e3146b7352ba279/T9166 was deleted AND the task/T9166 branch was destroyed.

The commit was orphaned in git's object database — recoverable via SHA but unreachable via any branch. Only luck that the orchestrator captured the SHA in verification metadata before cleanup ran.

CONTRAST: T9171 (Windows CRLF hotfix) completed under the same flow but its commits made it to release/v2026.5.50 and remotes/origin/task/T9171 persists. Different integration path? Need investigation.

DATA LOSS RISK: if a worker completes between cleo verify and cleanup, and the orchestrator doesn't immediately capture the SHA, that work is lost forever.

REPRO STEPS:
1. Spawn worker via cleo orchestrate spawn TXXXX
2. Worker commits to task/TXXXX in its worktree
3. Worker runs cleo verify --gate ... and cleo complete TXXXX
4. Observe: worktree directory deleted, branch task/TXXXX deleted
5. Try git log task/TXXXX → fails
6. The commit SHA is only retrievable from .verification.evidence.implemented metadata in tasks.db

This is a critical reliability defect for the orchestrator workflow.
acceptance: cleo complete <taskId> MUST NOT delete task/<id> branch from local repo until integration to base branch confirmed (or explicit --keep-branch flag)
cleo complete MUST NOT delete worktree directory until integration confirmed
If integration is async (e.g. via PR), branch must persist until merge
If integration is sync, complete waits for merge before cleanup
Documented integration path for orchestrator: how does task/<id> branch reach release/<version>? T9171 made it; T9166 did not — same code path?
Add CI/test gate: spawn worker, complete task, verify branch survives or commit is reachable on integration branch
Optional: cleo complete --recover-orphan auto-detects orphaned commits via reflog and offers to restore

tasks.title: v2026.5.51 Hotfix: NEXUS registration fails on pre-existing global nexus.db with is_external
tasks.description:
v2026.5.50 verification 2026-05-08 confirmed schema correctness on Linux+macOS but surfaced one remaining edge case: NEXUS project-registration code path doesn't share the duplicate-column tolerance that the full migration runner has.

ROOT CAUSE: T9164 added explicit forward migration for nexus_nodes.is_external. Fresh-init for a NEW project triggers nexus PROJECT REGISTRATION (against global ~/.local/share/cleo/nexus/nexus.db) which runs the migration. If that global nexus.db already has is_external from prior ensureColumns() safety-net repair, the ALTER TABLE fails with duplicate-column. The full migration runner uses isDuplicateColumnError + reconcileJournal Scenario 3 to handle this; the registration path apparently uses a different error handler.

EVIDENCE:
cleo init on new project against pre-existing global nexus.db emits:
'NEXUS registration: Failed to run the query 'ALTER TABLE nexus_nodes ADD COLUMN is_external integer NOT NULL DEFAULT false;'

INVESTIGATE: packages/core/src/nexus/ for the registerProject/runNexusMigrations call site that doesn't share the migration-manager's duplicate-column tolerance.

FIX: route registration migration calls through migrate-manager (migrateWithRetry/reconcileJournal) so the same duplicate-column handling applies.
acceptance: cleo init on fresh project against pre-existing global nexus.db (with is_external from ensureColumns) does NOT emit 'NEXUS registration: Failed to run the query ALTER TABLE nexus_nodes ADD COLUMN is_external' warning
Project-registration path catches duplicate-column SQLite errors via existing isDuplicateColumnError helper and treats them as success
Migrations on a freshly-cloned legacy nexus.db reconcile via reconcileJournal Scenario 3 same as brain.db does today
Existing nexus migration tests still pass
Repro test: pre-seed global nexus.db with ALTER TABLE adding is_external then run cleo init — must succeed silently

tasks.title: BUG EPIC: Stray .cleo/ and T### directories created outside registered project root
tasks.description: Production helpers create .cleo/ via mkdirSync without first calling assertProjectInitialized, and getCleoDirAbsolute(cwd) resolves relative paths against arbitrary cwd. Combined with SQLite auto-creating parent dirs on open, this produces stray .cleo/ directories anywhere a subagent happens to cd. Concrete evidence in /mnt/projects/cleocode root: T100/.cleo, T9086/.cleo/tasks.db (704KB), E001/.cleo, L21vY2svcHJvamVjdA/.cleo (base64url-decode = /mock/project — proving test fixture leakage), packages/core/T100, packages/core/E001, packages/core/.cleo/keys, packages/lafs/.cleo/tasks.db, dev/sandbox/.cleo, plus nested .cleo/council-runs/<run>/.cleo. Test fixtures in packages/cleo/src/dispatch/domains/__tests__/ use /mock/project literal as projectRoot; nexus.ts at lines 301,310,389,574,581,1249 base64url-encodes projectRoot and joins back into a real filesystem path. 10 unguarded mkdirSync sites identified. Single root cause = single fix: ensureCleoDir(projectRoot) chokepoint that calls assertProjectInitialized before mkdir.
acceptance: ensureCleoDir(projectRoot) chokepoint helper exists in packages/core/src/paths.ts and calls assertProjectInitialized before mkdirSync
All 10 identified unguarded mkdirSync(.cleo) sites migrated to the chokepoint
Test fixtures using /mock/project no longer leak directories to real filesystem
Repo-wide grep for mkdirSync.*\.cleo returns zero unguarded callers
CI regression guard: cleo invocation with cwd outside any registered project creates zero .cleo/ directories
Existing stray dirs audited for real data and removed

tasks.title: BUG EPIC: CLEO disk hygiene gaps — unbounded backup snapshots, orphaned worktrees, undrained quarantine
tasks.description: Multiple cleanup gaps causing 11GB project bloat and 15GB home bloat. (1) .cleo/backups/snapshot/ has 184 brain.db.snapshot files dating back to April (1.5GB) — system/backup.ts:118 createBackup has zero rotation. AGENTS.md docs claim 10-snapshot rotation but that only applies to vacuumIntoBackupAll path. (2) .cleo/backups/sqlite/ has 12 files (492MB) — rotation works but MAX_SNAPSHOTS=10 is hardcoded, regex excludes .db-journal siblings, race window between rotate-then-write. (3) ~/.local/share/cleo/worktrees/ = 15GB. 4 locked worktrees (T1825 T1875 T9165 T9175) never had cleo orchestrate worktree-complete invoked; pruneWorktree never ran. Each worktree carries its own 2.1GB node_modules — no shared pnpm store. 12 zero-byte sibling project-hash dirs are old hash-scheme orphans. (4) tasks-pre-cleo.db.bak / brain-pre-cleo.db.bak / tasks.db.PRE-DUP-FIX-* / .CORRUPTED-* / .SECOND-BROKEN-* (170MB) — automated writers bypass cleanup-legacy.ts:67 registry; PRE-DUP-FIX/CORRUPTED files are manual incident breadcrumbs with no source. (5) .cleo/quarantine/ (18MB) — rogue-cleo-detector.ts:332 writes here, no drain logic.
acceptance: system/backup.ts createBackup has rotation logic with configurable cap default 10 per file-prefix
sqlite-backup MAX_SNAPSHOTS configurable via config.json and journal siblings swept by same rotation
Locked worktrees never integrated still get pruned via post-completion hook or scheduled gc
Worktrees provisioned with shared pnpm store eliminating 2.1GB node_modules per worktree
cleo gc --quarantine drain command exists with N-day retention
cleanup-legacy.ts registry includes tasks-pre-cleo.db.bak and brain-pre-cleo.db.bak
pruneOrphanWorktrees sweeps empty parent project-hash dirs
One-shot cleanup of current bloat documented and run

tasks.title: BUG: dialectic.generate_object_failed (AI_APICallError on claude-sonnet-4-6) fires on every cleo command
tasks.description: Every cleo CLI invocation emits ERROR-level log spam: subsystem=dialectic, event=dialectic.generate_object_failed, errorCode=AI_APICallError or AI_RetryError, modelId=claude-sonnet-4-6, sessionId=ses_20260508142547_b345ae. The evaluator returns empty insights but the failure isn't graceful — it logs at ERROR on every single command. Likely causes: (a) Anthropic API key missing or rate-limited, (b) model claude-sonnet-4-6 unavailable on the configured provider/region, (c) request schema rejected by API. Discovered 2026-05-08 while filing T9193 + T9194.
acceptance: Root cause identified (auth, rate limit, schema, model availability)
Either fix the call or downgrade to non-blocking warn-once with cached fallback
Logs no longer spam ERROR level on every cleo invocation
Repro: 5 sequential cleo find foo invocations produce zero dialectic.generate_object_failed errors when healthy
Failure mode is graceful: empty insights returned no log spam
